title: Area vs Perimeter
subject: Math
intro_hook: If you need to fence a garden, do you calculate area or perimeter? What if you want to plant grass?
big_idea: Perimeter is the length of the outside edge, while Area is the space covered inside.

question_flow.q: If you are walking along the boundary of a playground, are you measuring area or perimeter?
If you want to lay tiles on the floor of a room, what are you measuring?
Can two shapes have the same perimeter but different areas?

indian_analogies: Buying wire to put a fence around a field (Perimeter).
Buying a carpet or durrie for a living room (Area).

misconceptions.belief: Perimeter and Area are the same thing.
misconceptions.probe: If I stretch a rubber band into a long thin rectangle, its perimeter stays the same, but does the space inside stay the same?

whiteboard.title: Area and Perimeter
whiteboard.goal: Distinguish between the boundary length and the surface space.
whiteboard.formula: Perimeter = Sum of all sides. Area of Rectangle = length × width.

symbols.means: Area (measured in sq cm, sq m)

steps.label: Identify Need
steps.detail: Is the problem about the boundary or the inside?

steps.label: Measure Sides
steps.detail: Find the lengths of the edges.

steps.label: Calculate P
steps.detail: Add all the outside edges together.

whiteboard.feedback_prompt: Can you tell me a real-life situation where you would need Area?
whiteboard.worked_example: Garden 5m long, 3m wide. Fence (Perimeter) = 5+5+3+3 = 16m. Grass (Area) = 5×3 = 15 sq meters.

common_confusions.fix: Perimeter is length (m). Area is squares (m²).
